title: ASP.NET Core Big Picture: A Practical Developer Guide
excerpt: A simple and practical overview of ASP.NET Core: what it is, how it works, MVC vs Razor Pages vs Blazor, Web API vs gRPC, and where SignalR fits.

body.value:
ASP.NET Core is Microsoft's web framework for building web applications on .NET.

The easiest way to understand it is this: it provides ready-to-use building blocks so you don’t have to implement everything from scratch.

It is not just about generating HTML pages. It supports multiple application styles including front-end UI, APIs, and real-time communication.

title: ASP.NET Core: How It Really Works (Simple Explanation)
excerpt: Understand what actually happens inside ASP.NET Core: request pipeline, middleware, Program.cs, and dependency injection.

value.title: Think in pipeline, not in controllers

body.value:
ASP.NET Core is not about controllers or pages.

It is a pipeline.

A request goes through steps and becomes a response.

value.caption: Simple mental model
value.diagram:
flowchart LR
A[Browser] --> B[Kestrel]
B --> C[Middleware]
C --> D[Routing]
D --> E[Controller/API]
E --> F[Response]

body.value:
Every ASP.NET Core app starts in Program.cs.

Two responsibilities:

  • Register services
  • Build request pipeline

value.caption: Minimal app

body.value:
Kestrel is the built-in web server.

It listens for HTTP requests.

Then it passes them into the pipeline.

Middleware are functions executed one after another.

Each middleware can:

  • Handle request
  • Modify request
  • Stop pipeline

value.caption: Middleware flow
value.diagram:
flowchart TD
A[Request] --> B[Middleware 1]
B --> C[Middleware 2]
C --> D[Middleware 3]
D --> E[Handler]
E --> F[Response]

body.value:
Example: static files.

If browser asks for image.jpg:

  • StaticFiles middleware finds it
  • Returns response immediately
  • Controller is skipped

ASP.NET Core creates objects for you.

You register services once:

value.caption: Use service

body.value:
• Singleton – one instance
  • Scoped – per request (most used)
  • Transient – always new

value.title: How to think about ASP.NET Core

body.value:
Do not think "controller handles request".

Think:

  • Kestrel receives request
  • Middleware pipeline processes it
  • Routing selects handler
  • Code runs
  • Response returns

This is the real model.
